channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: GraphQL vs REST vs tRPC: 2026’s API fight
item.description: Cut API design debates with data — REST vs GraphQL vs tRPC in 2026 with concrete benchmarks, cost figures, and the exact mistake I made shipping GraphQL for CRUD.

item.title: LLM latency: what kills speed first
item.description: Cut LLM latency 78%: the SDK knobs nobody tunes — connection pools, idle sockets, DNS cache, and the first-token cache you missed

item.title: Stay relevant when tools change faster than you can
item.description: Stay technically relevant in 2026 by classifying tools into 3 tiers and spending your learning budget wisely — benchmarks, rollback plans, and Renovate automation included.

item.title: Killed $3k Kubernetes, kept $200 infra
item.description: Cut AWS infrastructure costs 86% by replacing $3k/month Kubernetes with ECS Fargate, CloudFront, and Redis — real numbers, real stack, real mistakes.

item.title: AI-proof your salary: 5 skills that still pay 2026
item.description: Cut API response time 60% and protect your 2026 salary with 5 boring but proven skills — connection pooling, observability, cache hit rate, cold starts, and cost per request. Real metrics, code, and mistakes included.

item.title: React 19 compiler: 3x faster re-renders or bust?
item.description: Cut React 19 re-renders 61% with the compiler — build times, TTI drops, and the TypeScript gotcha most docs miss.

item.title: Enforce SBOM in 30 mins: Syft + Trivy 2026
item.description: Enforce SBOM in 30 mins using Syft 0.10.0 and Trivy 0.48.3 — cut audit rework 83%, fail builds on invalid SPDX, and publish SBOMs to GCS for 0.035 USD per build.

item.title: SaaS in 6 weeks: 80% AI code failed in prod
item.description: Ship a real SaaS in 6 weeks using AI tools — but only if you control the architecture. Learn the exact constraints, benchmarks, and refactor patterns that separate working code from melted VPS instances.

item.title: 2026 GitOps showdown: ArgoCD vs Flux after two years
item.description: Cut Kubernetes drift 90% with GitOps — Flux v2.3 syncs in 390 ms, costs 0.9 vCPU, and runs for two years without a UI meltdown. Real production numbers from 2026.

item.title: Deploy a solo stack for $20/month in 2026
item.description: A simple endpoint took 45ms on x86 and 38ms on ARM64.

item.title: Land $5k remote roles: what Nairobi devs got right
item.description: Land $5k remote roles in 2026 — how Nairobi and Lagos devs reframed their résumés, built async portfolios, and closed seed-stage contracts at the top of the range.

item.title: Zero-downtime migrations: let the DB do the work
item.description: Cut migration downtime 90% by letting PostgreSQL do the work — backfill in batches, dual writes, and materialized views you can roll back in seconds

item.title: AI killed these Micro-SaaS niches
item.description: The only thing it couldn’t do was handle the 1,000+ concurrent users I’d planned for.

item.title: 7 AI red flags your senior title hides
item.description: Cut AI hallucinations 68% with Sentry AI Guard 2.4 — the only tool that enforces senior-level quality in CI without rewriting your stack

item.title: Skip the zero-trust hype for 5-person teams
item.description: Cut security overhead 60% with minimal zero trust — secrets rotation, rate limits, and when to skip mTLS. Tools: Vault 1.15, Cognito, Calico 3.26.

item.title: AI code traps: Copilot vs Cursor smackdown
item.description: Cut AI code crashes 40% by picking the right sidekick. Compare GitHub Copilot Enterprise 2026 vs Cursor v0.32 on latency, edge cases, and offline survival.

item.title: 6 ways to ship real-time without WebSockets
item.description: Cut API response time 60% with SSE in 2026 — connection pooling, keep-alive tricks, and the one mistake most teams make with WebSockets. Includes cost breakdown per 1M updates.

item.title: 4 AI tools shipping real products in 2026
item.description: Ship real products faster with 4 AI tools tested in 2026 — cost, latency, and setup time for non-traditional developers building compliance APIs, SaaS, and more.

item.title: Pass interviews without a CS degree
item.description: Self-taught devs pass remote interviews by debugging real systems, not just LeetCode. Learn the trade-offs interviewers actually test — latency budgets, memory leaks, cache stampedes — with specific tools, benchmarks, and fixes.

item.title: Cut latency 70% with edge functions or waste CPU
item.description: Cut API response time 70% with edge functions — real p50/p95 numbers, Cloudflare Workers vs Vercel Edge pros/cons, and the exact TTL mistake most teams make.
